name: Thatch bundle
description: Dry stalks pulled from the meadow and tied tight. Rope, roofing, pillow fill, or a quick fire wrap.

name: Grass fiber
description: Tough green strands stripped from living blades. Bandages, nets, trap string.

name: Soft down
description: Seed fluff that sticks to your sleeve if you brush the seed heads. Soft lining for pouches, fever cloth, and bedding.

name: Chirper shell
description: Empty cricket shell, still faintly buzzing in your head. Good bait, or crush it for grit.

name: Meadow mite husk
description: Papery mite shell left in the thatch. Grind it into binder for plague and antiserum work.

name: Wild oat head
description: A grain spike knocked loose while parting the grass. Mash, travel cake, or animal feed.

name: Knotweed stem
description: A bendy stem that springs back when you cut it. Holds as a splint stick or basket rib.

name: Dew-caught seed
description: A seed still wet with morning dew. Pocket it for a later field sow.

name: Lost stitch scrap
description: Frayed cloth snagged on a stem, a few stitches still holding. Cheap dressing backing.

name: Field snail trail
description: Sticky trail goo scraped off a leaf. Binds salves and drawing poultices.

name: Burrow fluff
description: Warm nest lining kicked free of a burrow mouth. Tinder, smoke packing, or soft fill when better cloth is dear.

name: Rusted clasp
description: A corroded buckle pin lost in the grass years ago. Scrap metal for traps and repair kits.
